creator: kyle.a.taylor@gmail.com
text:
I wish. I use this toolchain everyday. If I install gdal, py39-gdal, and py39-numpy from pkg, the tooling fails. 

If I build from source from ports with numpy support enabled in py39-gdal, everything works just fine. It's not that big a deal that I have to build from ports; a minor annoyance. But a new FreeBSD user just installing gdal / py-gdal from pkg will wonder why the tooling appears broken out-of-the-box.

Example:

####

# bastille create gdal-test 14.0-RELEASE *** em0
Valid: (***).
Valid: (em0).

Creating a thinjail...

[gdal-test]:
gdal-test: created

[gdal-test]:
Applying template: default/thin...
[gdal-test]:
Applying template: default/base...
[gdal-test]:
[gdal-test]: 0
...

# bastille pkg gdal-test install -y gdal py39-gdal py39-numpy
[gdal-test]:
Bootstrapping pkg from pkg+http://pkg.FreeBSD.org/FreeBSD:14:amd64/quarterly, please wait...
Verifying signature with trusted certificate pkg.freebsd.org.2013102301... done
[gdal-test] Installing pkg-1.20.9...
[gdal-test] Extracting pkg-1.20.9: 100%
Updating FreeBSD repository catalogue...
[gdal-test] Fetching meta.conf: 100%    163 B   0.2kB/s    00:01
[gdal-test] Fetching packagesite.pkg: 100%    7 MiB   7.2MB/s    00:01
Processing entries: 100%
FreeBSD repository update completed. 33716 packages processed.
All repositories are up to date.
Updating database digests format: 100%
The following 99 package(s) will be affected (of 0 checked):
...

# bastille console gdal-test
[gdal-test]:
root@gdal-test:~ # gdal_calc.py --help
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/gdal_calc.py", line 8, in <module>
    from osgeo_utils.gdal_calc import *  # noqa
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/osgeo_utils/gdal_calc.py", line 46, in <module>
    from osgeo import gdal, gdal_array
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/osgeo/gdal_array.py", line 10, in <module>
    from . import _gdal_array
ImportError: cannot import name '_gdal_array' from 'osgeo' (/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/osgeo/__init__.py)
